tool: hugin-tools cpfind --fullscale / cpclean / staged autooptimiser on Siril-autostretched calibrated frames; hfov pinned at the solved value

centre_shift_informational_px.note: reported only — carrying it needs lensfun's undocumented <center> element

accepted_by: star_stations + seqtilt A/B on a real stack, never this fit's own residual
hfov_source: solved (solve_field.py on a set-01 CFA frame): scale 18.0725 arcsec/px -> 30.4421 deg (nominal EXIF 28.6 deg was NOT used; the optimizer pins hfov)

displacement_field_diff.note: lensfun normalization r=1 at corner (half-diagonal 3643 px); at r=0.9 (~12 px of correction) the two models differ by only 0.2 px

refit_verdict.hypothesis: x86 hugin 2025.0.1 reproduces the arm-era fitted a/b/c so the carried-over model stands
refit_verdict.result: CONFIRMED — the two independent fits agree to sub-pixel (a,b within 3-4%; c tiny absolute; max displacement diff 0.47 px mid-field, 0.34 px RMS). Difference is within fit noise (different 12-frame sample + correspondence set + hfov 30.44 vs arm ~30.35).
refit_verdict.decision: INCUMBENT STANDS — the new coefficients are a lateral, sub-pixel move, not a measurable improvement; re-installing would change the production-validated deliverable by <=0.47 px with no benefit. The per-rig re-fit obligation (removal register) is now SATISFIED.
refit_verdict.caveat: A model REPLACEMENT would still require the doc-mandated star_stations + seqtilt A/B on a real stack (never the fit residual) + user gate; not warranted here given sub-pixel equivalence.
